qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step when translating a page?
qa_pairs.answer: First, one writes the content in the original language.

qa_pairs.question: What is the second step in the page translation process?
qa_pairs.answer: The page is marked for translation.

qa_pairs.question: Who translates the page content?
qa_pairs.answer: A human translates the page.

qa_pairs.question: What wiki extension provides tools for human translation?
qa_pairs.answer: The wiki extension Translate provides the organizational tools for human translation.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one find tutorials for the Translate extension?
qa_pairs.answer: Tutorials for the Translate extension can be found at https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Extension:Translate.

qa_pairs.question: What is the final step in the translation process after a human translates the page?
qa_pairs.answer: A second human reviews the translation.

qa_pairs.question: What do users see if a page has not yet been translated?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can see the page in the original language if it has not yet been translated.

qa_pairs.question: What do users see if a translation has not yet been reviewed?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can see the non-reviewed translation.

qa_pairs.question: What happens when a page is marked for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Marking a page for translation will trigger an analysis of the content of the wiki page.

qa_pairs.question: How does the wiki extension split the page content?
qa_pairs.answer: The page content will be split by the extension into so-called translation units.

qa_pairs.question: What are some examples of translation units?
qa_pairs.answer: Translation units can be a title, a paragraph, an image, or similar small units.

qa_pairs.question: What is the benefit of translating content in small units?
qa_pairs.answer: Translating small units ensures that a modification to a page does not trigger the translation of the whole page.

qa_pairs.question: What information can be tracked using translation units?
qa_pairs.answer: Translation units allow tracking of what percentage of a page is translated or outdated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to mark a new page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Ensure that the wiki code to be translated is enclosed within <translate> </translate> tags.

qa_pairs.question: Which tag must appear at the very top of a new page to be marked for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: The tag <languages /> must appear at the very top of the page.

qa_pairs.question: How do you access the option to mark a new page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Go into “View” mode, and then click on the “Mark this page for translation” option.

qa_pairs.question: What should be done after clicking 'Mark this page for translation'?
qa_pairs.answer: Review the translation units.

qa_pairs.question: How can you prevent wiki code (like tables or tags) from being translated when marking a new page?
qa_pairs.answer: This can be done by breaking the page into multiple <translate> </translate> sections.

qa_pairs.question: What are the acceptable priority languages when marking a new page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: You should write either “fr” or “en” as the priority language.

qa_pairs.question: What is the final action to mark a new version of a page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on “Mark this version for translation”.

qa_pairs.question: When is it recommended to mark a page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to mark a page for translation only once it is stable.

qa_pairs.question: What tags should not be changed when making edits to an already translated page?
qa_pairs.answer: You should not change the tags of the form <!--T:3--> as they are automatically generated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to mark changes to an already translated page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Ensure that the new text to be translated is enclosed within <translate> </translate> tags.

qa_pairs.question: What text indicates that a translated page has changes that need to be marked?
qa_pairs.answer: In “View” mode, you should see the text “This page has changes since it was last marked for translation.” at the top of the page.

qa_pairs.question: What should you click on if a translated page has changes that need to be marked?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on “marked for translation” when you see the message indicating changes.

qa_pairs.question: How do you prevent wiki code from being translated when marking changes to a page?
qa_pairs.answer: You can prevent wiki code from being translated by breaking the page into multiple <translate> </translate> sections.

qa_pairs.question: What priority languages can be selected when marking changes to a page?
qa_pairs.answer: Either “fr” or “en” can be written as the priority language.
